meta.title: Impact of Online Gaming on Governor Landry's 2027 Prospects
meta.subtitle: Testing the political effect of signing an online gaming compact and delivering voter priorities versus inaction

feedback_callout.heading: Share Your Feedback
feedback_callout.body: Have questions about this experiment-effects report or ideas for future analysis? We'd love to hear from you.

turnout_assumption.note: Adjust the turnout-rate assumption. The default uses the current poststratified expected-turnout topline. Key Findings and summary tooltips use modeled poststratified denominators from weights.rds for the matching geography or subgroup.

denominator_sources.cultural_regions: weights.rds::toplines.expected_voters rolled up from county totals via county_culture_lookup.rds

key_takeaways: This page reports modeled treatment effects from a two-arm randomized scenario design in Louisiana.
Arm A combines policy action with delivery: Governor Landry signs an online casino compact and uses revenue to deliver each respondent's top-ranked priority before 2027.
Arm B is an inaction/no-delivery scenario: by 2027, Governor Landry has not delivered the respondent's top-ranked priority.
methodology_note: Respondents were randomly assigned to one of two scenarios. Arm A asked respondents to imagine Governor Jeff Landry signs an agreement authorizing online casino gaming in Louisiana and uses new revenue to deliver their top-ranked priority before the 2027 election. Arm B asked respondents to imagine that by the 2027 election, Governor Landry has not delivered their top-ranked priority. Within each arm, post-scenario outcomes are compared to that arm's own pre-scenario baseline. The difference-in-differences estimand compares those two changes. Estimates are modeled and poststratified, and are shown in percentage points.

response_universe.note: With-view effects exclude undecided/no-opinion responses before computing pre/post changes. Vote equivalents remain illustrative and use poststratified expected-voter denominators.

modes.label: What respondents with a view said

archetype_metadata.label: Soccer Moms
archetype_metadata.demographic_composition: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
archetype_metadata.rationale: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

tooltip.title: Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Suburban, College completed or Graduate degree, White
tooltip.body: The single most-watched voter group in American politics since the 1990s. Swung toward Democrats in 2018/2020, shifted back in parts of 2024. Their education level, suburban geography, and sensitivity to tone/culture-war issues make them the ultimate bellwether for competitive House and Senate races nationwide.

archetype_metadata.label: White Working Class
archetype_metadata.rationale: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

tooltip.title: White Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: White, No college
tooltip.body: The realignment story of the last decade. This group's shift from Obama to Trump remade the electoral map. Any erosion or consolidation in this bloc makes front-page news because it directly determines outcomes in PA, MI, WI, and Sun Belt exurbs.

archetype_metadata.label: Barstool Conservatives
archetype_metadata.demographic_composition: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
archetype_metadata.rationale: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

tooltip.title: Barstool Conservatives
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen X or Millennials, No college or College completed, Republican or Other party
tooltip.body: Culturally conservative but socially libertarian — anti-woke but pro-choice, pro-legal weed, pro-gambling. Credited with helping propel Trump to victory in 2024 through alternative media (Rogan, Barstool Sports, UFC). Not church-right but vibes-right. Their turnout and enthusiasm is a leading indicator of populist energy, and both parties are now scrambling to understand them.

archetype_metadata.label: Disaffected Young Men
archetype_metadata.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
archetype_metadata.rationale: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

tooltip.title: Disaffected Young Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation
tooltip.body: The emerging wildcard of American elections. Low-propensity, politically homeless, over-indexed on alternative media. Both parties competing for them. Their turnout alone can shift margins by 2-4 points in swing states.

archetype_metadata.label: Black Women of Faith
archetype_metadata.rationale: The most reliable voting bloc in the Democratic coalition. When their turnout dips even slightly, Democrats lose. Media tracks their enthusiasm as a proxy for the health of the entire Democratic operation.

tooltip.title: Black Women of Faith
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Black, Protestant, Democrat
tooltip.body: The most reliable voting bloc in the Democratic coalition. When their turnout dips even slightly, Democrats lose. Media tracks their enthusiasm as a proxy for the health of the entire Democratic operation.

archetype_metadata.label: Hispanic Working Class
archetype_metadata.rationale: The fastest-moving demographic in American politics. Their rightward shift in 2020 and 2024 caught both parties off guard. This group's trajectory determines whether the Sun Belt stays competitive or locks in for one party.

tooltip.title: Hispanic Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Hispanic, No college
tooltip.body: The fastest-moving demographic in American politics. Their rightward shift in 2020 and 2024 caught both parties off guard. This group's trajectory determines whether the Sun Belt stays competitive or locks in for one party.

archetype_metadata.label: Evangelical Republicans
archetype_metadata.rationale: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

tooltip.title: Evangelical Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Protestant
tooltip.body: The floor of the Republican coalition. Their mobilization determines whether GOP candidates hit their targets in virtually every state. Post-Dobbs, media watches whether this group stays energized or fractures over candidate quality.

archetype_metadata.label: MAGA Country
archetype_metadata.rationale: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

tooltip.title: MAGA Country
tooltip.subtitle: Rural, Republican, White, No college
tooltip.body: The margin machine for Republicans. Their ever-increasing turnout in non-metro areas offsets Democratic gains in suburbs. Media covers them as the cultural heartbeat of the populist right. Any sign of turnout fatigue here is a major story.

archetype_metadata.label: TikTok Left
archetype_metadata.rationale: The energy wing of the Democratic base. Their enthusiasm on climate, student debt, and social issues translates to volunteer hours, small-dollar donations, and turnout in college towns. When disengaged, Democratic margins collapse.

tooltip.title: TikTok Left
tooltip.subtitle: Gen Z, Democrat
tooltip.body: The energy wing of the Democratic base. Their enthusiasm on climate, student debt, and social issues translates to volunteer hours, small-dollar donations, and turnout in college towns. When disengaged, Democratic margins collapse.

archetype_metadata.label: Costco Centrists
archetype_metadata.rationale: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

tooltip.title: Costco Centrists
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Other party affiliation
tooltip.body: The persuadable middle that both parties need. Not ideological, but they vote. Media uses them as the 'normal person' stand-in — decides based on gas prices, grocery costs, and vibes, not policy papers.

archetype_metadata.label: Tradwives
archetype_metadata.demographic_composition: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
archetype_metadata.rationale: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

tooltip.title: Tradwives
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Millennials or Gen Z, Republican, Protestant or Catholic, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: A culturally ascendant archetype driven by social media. Embraces traditional gender roles, homemaking aesthetics, and conservative family values. Gained massive visibility in 2024 as a cultural counterpoint to progressive feminism. Media watches them because the tradwife trend was seen as a leading indicator of Trump's win among white women.

archetype_metadata.label: Young Black Men
archetype_metadata.rationale: A small but symbolically enormous group. Any movement away from Democrats gets massive media coverage because it threatens a core assumption of the Democratic coalition. Even marginal shifts carry outsize narrative weight.

tooltip.title: Young Black Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Black, Gen Z
tooltip.body: A small but symbolically enormous group. Any movement away from Democrats gets massive media coverage because it threatens a core assumption of the Democratic coalition. Even marginal shifts carry outsize narrative weight.

archetype_metadata.label: Country Club Republicans
archetype_metadata.demographic_composition: Republican, Suburban, Rich (125k+), College completed or Graduate degree
archetype_metadata.rationale: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

tooltip.title: Country Club Republicans
tooltip.subtitle: Republican, Suburban, Rich (125k+), College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The Republican voters most likely to defect on candidate quality. Their movement in wealthy suburbs (Bucks County, Maricopa, Gwinnett) decided multiple swing states. If they hold for the GOP, the party is strong.

archetype_metadata.label: Latte Liberals
archetype_metadata.rationale: The ideological anchor of the Democratic Party. Highly engaged, high-turnout, and the source of most progressive policy energy. When fired up, Democratic campaigns have money and volunteers.

tooltip.title: Latte Liberals
tooltip.subtitle: Democrat, Urban, Graduate/professional degree
tooltip.body: The ideological anchor of the Democratic Party. Highly engaged, high-turnout, and the source of most progressive policy energy. When fired up, Democratic campaigns have money and volunteers.

archetype_metadata.label: Hispanic Soccer Moms
archetype_metadata.rationale: An emerging swing bloc. More moderate than progressive activists, more engaged than working-class Hispanic men. As Sun Belt suburbs diversify, this group increasingly determines outcomes in AZ, NV, and TX.

tooltip.title: Hispanic Soccer Moms
tooltip.subtitle: Female, Hispanic, Suburban, College completed
tooltip.body: An emerging swing bloc. More moderate than progressive activists, more engaged than working-class Hispanic men. As Sun Belt suburbs diversify, this group increasingly determines outcomes in AZ, NV, and TX.

archetype_metadata.label: Tech Bros
archetype_metadata.demographic_composition: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation, Above median or Rich income
archetype_metadata.rationale: A new force in campaign finance and online discourse. Mobilized around deregulation, crypto, and anti-establishment messaging. Their money and meme energy can shift narratives quickly.

tooltip.title: Tech Bros
tooltip.subtitle: Male, Gen Z or Millennials, Other party affiliation, Above median or Rich income
tooltip.body: A new force in campaign finance and online discourse. Mobilized around deregulation, crypto, and anti-establishment messaging. Their money and meme energy can shift narratives quickly.

archetype_metadata.label: Kennedy Catholics
archetype_metadata.rationale: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

tooltip.title: Kennedy Catholics
tooltip.subtitle: Catholic, Suburban or Urban
tooltip.body: The original swing demographic in American politics. White Catholics lean R, Hispanic Catholics are contested. Cross-pressured on abortion, immigration, and social welfare. Distributed across every swing state.

archetype_metadata.label: Red State Women
archetype_metadata.demographic_composition: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
archetype_metadata.rationale: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

tooltip.title: Red State Women
tooltip.subtitle: Female, White, No college, Republican, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: Often overlooked in media's focus on college-educated suburban women. They are a massive and growing part of the Republican base. Their concerns — inflation, safety, family affordability — drive GOP messaging. Trump gained among them in 2024.

archetype_metadata.label: Boomer Democrats
archetype_metadata.demographic_composition: Boomers+, Democrat, College completed or Graduate degree
archetype_metadata.rationale: The reliable turnout machine of the Democratic Party — they actually show up in midterms. Often overlooked for flashier demographics, but their consistency keeps Democrats competitive in off-year elections.

tooltip.title: Boomer Democrats
tooltip.subtitle: Boomers+, Democrat, College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The reliable turnout machine of the Democratic Party — they actually show up in midterms. Often overlooked for flashier demographics, but their consistency keeps Democrats competitive in off-year elections.

archetype_metadata.label: Blue-Collar Men
archetype_metadata.demographic_composition: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
archetype_metadata.rationale: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

tooltip.title: Blue-Collar Men
tooltip.subtitle: Male, White, No college, Poor or Below median income, Suburban or Rural
tooltip.body: The kitchen-table economics voter. Cares about wages, trade, and cost of living more than culture war. Overlaps with but is distinct from Barstool Conservatives — these guys are family-first, not libertine. Their economic anxiety is a recurring media theme.

archetype_metadata.label: GenX Forgotten Middle
archetype_metadata.rationale: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

tooltip.title: GenX Forgotten Middle
tooltip.subtitle: GenX, Other party affiliation, Suburban
tooltip.body: Perpetually overlooked in media that ping-pongs between Boomers and Gen Z. But GenX is in peak earning and voting years, and their independent streak makes them genuinely persuadable.

archetype_metadata.label: Secular Suburbanites
archetype_metadata.demographic_composition: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
archetype_metadata.rationale: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

tooltip.title: Secular Suburbanites
tooltip.subtitle: Suburban, Not religious, College completed or Graduate degree
tooltip.body: The fastest-growing religious demographic is no religion at all. As 'nones' concentrate in suburbs, they look like a coherent voting bloc — socially liberal, fiscally moderate, allergic to culture-war messaging.

archetype_metadata.label: Poor Rural Democrats
archetype_metadata.rationale: A shrinking but real constituency — complicates the narrative that rural = Republican. Concentrated in the Deep South Black Belt, Appalachian pockets, and Native communities. Mobilization matters in close Senate races.

tooltip.title: Poor Rural Democrats
tooltip.subtitle: Democrat, Rural, Poor (<35k)
tooltip.body: A shrinking but real constituency — complicates the narrative that rural = Republican. Concentrated in the Deep South Black Belt, Appalachian pockets, and Native communities. Mobilization matters in close Senate races.

archetype_metadata.label: Six-Figure Socialists
archetype_metadata.rationale: The fundraising engine of the progressive movement. Their policy preferences drive Democratic platform priorities disproportionately. Their disconnect from working-class voters is itself a recurring story.

tooltip.title: Six-Figure Socialists
tooltip.subtitle: Democrat, Urban, Rich (125k+), Graduate/professional degree
tooltip.body: The fundraising engine of the progressive movement. Their policy preferences drive Democratic platform priorities disproportionately. Their disconnect from working-class voters is itself a recurring story.

archetype_metadata.label: Black Rural Working Class
archetype_metadata.rationale: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

tooltip.title: Black Rural Working Class
tooltip.subtitle: Black - rural - no college
tooltip.body: An overlooked but consequential bloc in the rural South and Black Belt. Their political weight can be felt in tight races across multiple Southern states.

notes.pre_post_levels_note: With-view effects exclude undecided/no-opinion responses before computing pre/post changes. Vote equivalents remain illustrative and use poststratified expected-voter denominators.
notes.combined_interval_note: Combined uncertainty intervals are shown only when direct combined rows exist. Where support/favorability combined metrics are derived by summing component effects, combined intervals are omitted because response-level covariances are not available.
notes.uncertainty_note: Uncertainty columns are shown when available from the modeled experiment outputs. Derived net rows may not have covariance-aware intervals in this prototype build.
